project_judgment.primary_structural_problem: the repo has established document truth but no formed execution surface yet
project_judgment.why_this_path: the repo already has product or architecture facts, so Loom should preserve those facts and add only a lightweight governance loop until execution intent is explicit

scaffold_profile.name: execution-control
scaffold_profile.description: Loom-owned work item, progress, review, status, and spec carriers

deferred_capabilities.name: strong-governance-host-gates
deferred_capabilities.reason: execution-control writes Loom-owned execution carriers but does not require host merge and closeout gates as a profile precondition
deferred_capabilities.upgrade_trigger: host required checks, controlled merge, or closeout reconciliation become part of the adoption contract

initial_work_items.goal: Bootstrap the first executable Loom path for this repository
initial_work_items.scope: Establish rule entry, first work item, progress carrier, spec/plan, and verification entry
initial_work_items.closing_condition: The generated entry, work item, recovery entry, and templates are readable and verified
initial_work_items.post_build_continuation: Promote the first real downstream issue after the bootstrap artifacts are accepted
initial_work_items.owner_for_checkpoint_lite: repository owner or current bootstrap operator

checkpoint_relationship: admission checkpoint confirms the bootstrap work item and first artifacts are readable
build checkpoint confirms generated carriers and templates are internally consistent
merge checkpoint should only pass after downstream repo truth, docs, and delivery state align
validation_and_closing.clean_state: all generated Loom artifacts are readable, verified, and free of conflicting duplicates
close_when: the target repo has a readable root or companion Loom entry
the first work item, progress carrier, and spec/plan artifacts exist
the bootstrap manifest and init-result are verifiable

runtime_state.summary: runtime carrier `installed-skills-root` is executing as `installed-runtime` with a consistent bundled runtime.

carrier_layout.summary: carrier layout exposes an installed skills root and shared runtime tree.

registry_contract.summary: registry, upgrade contract, and skill entrypoints are consistent.

shared_runtime.summary: shared runtime scripts are present and executable roots can be resolved.

referenced_resources.summary: shared references and assets required by the installed runtime are present.

repair.summary: remove blanket .loom ignore and keep only runtime/cache scratch paths ignored

ci_check_presence.recommended_action: install the workflow and configure its stable check names as host-required checks

budget.summary: execution budget is not collected for this invocation.

release_targets.summary: no target repository release/version surface is declared for this repository.

target_release.summary: no target repository release/version surface is declared for this repository.

tool_availability.summary: no dynamic tools are declared for this repository.

policy_readiness.summary: no approval or sandbox policy read surfaces are declared for this repository.

hook_profile.summary: hooks extension profile is not enabled for this repository.

repo_interface.summary: no repo companion interface is declared for this repository.

repo_interop.summary: no repo interop contract is declared for this repository.

single-workspace.summary: Use the repository root as the Loom execution workspace.
single-workspace.recommended_action: keep workspace_entry as `.` unless isolation becomes necessary

per-item-worktree.summary: Use one isolated workspace per Work Item, usually backed by host git worktree.
per-item-worktree.recommended_action: ensure workspace, branch, Work Item, and PR bindings stay aligned

attach-existing.summary: Attach Loom to an existing repo-defined workspace without taking over host lifecycle actions.
attach-existing.recommended_action: declare the repo-specific workspace locator and keep host lifecycle ownership external

workspace_profile.recommended_action: restore the Work Item workspace_entry before running workspace profile checks

gate_starter.recommended_action: install or refresh repo-local Loom runtime entries before using gate starter aliases

single-workspace.summary: Use the repository root as the Loom execution workspace.

per-item-worktree.summary: Use one isolated workspace per Work Item, usually backed by host git worktree.

workspace_profile.recommended_action: restore the Work Item workspace_entry before running workspace profile checks

gate_starter.recommended_action: install or refresh repo-local Loom runtime entries before using gate starter aliases

phase.locator: GitHub parent issue or equivalent planning object

fr.locator: GitHub sub-issue or equivalent formal request

implementation_pr.locator: GitHub PR linked from Work Item or branch

host_binding.summary: host binding surface is readable, but required binding facts are missing or host-managed.

taxonomy.spec_stale: Approved spec no longer covers the implementation surface.
taxonomy.review_stale: Implementation review no longer covers the current head or scope.
taxonomy.head_drift: The head SHA changed after an approval gate was recorded.
taxonomy.host_signal_drift: GitHub issue, PR, project, branch, or check state no longer agrees with Loom's local carriers.
taxonomy.gate_failure: A required predecessor gate is missing, blocking, or unreadable.
taxonomy.closeout_reconciliation_drift: Merged work and issue/project closeout state are not yet aligned.

blockers.reason: required light maturity carriers are missing or unreadable

evidence.locator: GitHub PR linked from Work Item or branch

judgment.summary: GitHub profile maturity is blocked by unreadable or conflicting required signals.

light.summary: Minimal Work Item -> review -> merge-ready governance is available.

standard.summary: Formal spec, spec gate, status control plane, basic host binding, and closeout/reconciliation reads are available.

strong.summary: Host-backed binding, reconciliation, controlled merge, and closeout gates are available.

light.recommended_action: run loom-init or restore the Work Item carrier

standard.recommended_action: declare the FR -> Work Item split through the GitHub profile upgrade path
record or restore the spec review gate
run loom_status or loom_check to rebuild the status control plane
run governance-profile binding and repair missing host bindings
install repo interop so closeout can consume reconciliation

strong.recommended_action: install or repair the repo companion interface
install or repair the repo interop contract
enable controlled merge binding and required host gates
verify branch protection or ruleset enforcement plus required checks before claiming strong governance
prove the PR merge path is host-owned and readable before strong governance
prove controlled merge depends on verified host checks instead of local aliases
prove closeout consumes merge commit and reconciliation basis before strong governance

light.recommended_action: run loom-init or restore the Work Item carrier

standard.recommended_action: declare the FR -> Work Item split through the GitHub profile upgrade path
record or restore the spec review gate
run governance-profile binding and repair missing host bindings
install repo interop so closeout can consume reconciliation

strong.recommended_action: install or repair the repo companion interface
install or repair the repo interop contract
enable controlled merge binding and required host gates
verify branch protection or ruleset enforcement plus required checks before claiming strong governance
prove controlled merge depends on verified host checks instead of local aliases
prove closeout consumes merge commit and reconciliation basis before strong governance

advisory.summary: Default mode for newly adopted repositories; Loom reports gate signals without becoming the blocking authority.

blocking.summary: Explicit opt-in mode for strong governance repositories after adversarial adoption checks pass.

rollback.summary: Emergency switch back to advisory consumption when runtime, evidence, or host bindings drift.

blocking_preconditions.recommended_action: upgrade the repository to strong maturity before enabling blocking gates
run the Loom-owned strong-governance adversarial adoption fixture and record the validation evidence
keep rollback available by switching gate mode back to advisory and rerunning governance-profile status

rollback.recommended_action: disable blocking consumption, preserve evidence, repair drift, then rerun adversarial adoption checks before re-enabling blocking

conditions.signal: runtime-state or runtime parity fails for the installed Loom runtime
conditions.recommended_action: restore the last known-good runtime or rebootstrap before consuming blocking gates again

conditions.signal: version-controlled evidence locators are missing, stale, or no longer match the generated profile plan
conditions.recommended_action: repair evidence carriers and rerun adversarial adoption checks before re-enabling blocking

conditions.signal: GitHub issue, PR, branch, project, merge commit, or branch-protection bindings no longer match the recorded profile state
conditions.recommended_action: return to advisory and reconcile host bindings before consuming gate results as blocking

conditions.signal: spec or implementation review records no longer bind to the expected review head
conditions.recommended_action: refresh review records against the current head and repeat the gate rollout check

conditions.signal: Work Item, Project, PR, or companion metadata cannot be parsed into the expected Loom contracts
conditions.recommended_action: repair metadata parsing or carrier format before restoring blocking consumption

governance_surface.summary: resume chain is only partially supported because governance carriers or GitHub control-plane signals are incomplete.
missing_inputs: no stable Loom carriers are readable yet

create.semantics: establish or confirm the workspace_entry execution workspace

locate.semantics: resolve workspace_entry, recovery_entry, checkpoint, and purity without mutation

attach.semantics: locate and bind an existing repo-defined workspace

handoff.semantics: consume the same workspace/recovery contract and preserve recovery authored fields

cleanup.semantics: remove only explicit Loom-owned temporary residue

retire.semantics: record local-only retire evidence without writing versioned recovery or status carriers

remove.fallback_to: workspace cleanup/retire plus host-owned directory or git worktree lifecycle

execution_boundary.run: read/event surface only; Loom does not start a daemon
execution_boundary.stop: read/event surface only; Loom does not own worker termination

worker_backend.future_backend_rule: future backends may change invocation mechanics but must preserve Work Item, workspace, recovery, and ledger truth boundaries

missing_details.recommended_action: run loom-init or restore the Work Item carrier

intentionally_absent.reason: release target truth stays absent until the repo declares release target intent

repo_owned_residue.summary: root rules and repo-native validation remain repo-owned
